name: lparstats
explanation: The LPARStats check shells out to the AIX `lparstat` CLI (`/usr/bin/lparstat`, optionally via `sudo`) and parses its tabular output for memory, hypervisor, and SPURR processor utilization metrics. There is no network endpoint, no credential, and no required field — discovery is purely host-local: "is this an AIX LPAR with `lparstat` runnable?". This is pattern L ("Local CLI subprocess"): the Agent host either has the binary installed (AIX-only) or it doesn't. Several optional sub-collectors (`memory_entitlements`, `hypervisor`) require root or a sudoers entry for `dd-agent`, which is an environmental privilege constraint rather than a credential the auto-config layer could discover.
notes: Pattern L analogue, AIX-only. Auto-config trigger: detect AIX OS plus presence and runnability of `/usr/bin/lparstat`. No probe target; method `other`. Privilege-gated sub-features (sudo/root) are out of scope for discovery.
